title: ADP provenance part (layer 2)
description: Where the canonical timeline came from. Two levels: `sources[]` describes each raw source (device, file, time window, clock-sync, dual-HR anchor, sha256 content hash); `canonical` describes each merged timeline column (which source it came from, gap classification, coverage, longest gap). Grounded in rfr validation.json.

sources.description: Per raw source that fed the timeline.

canonical.description: Per canonical timeline column: its provenance and coverage against the source that fed it. Keys are canonical column names (lat, lon, alt_m, speed_ms, hr_bpm, power_w, cadence_rpm, distance_m, moving, grit, flow, ...).

diagnostics.description: OPTIONAL cross-source merge-quality diagnostics: deterministic refine-time facts about how well the sources agreed when merged onto the canonical timeline. These belong to provenance (they describe the quality of the merge that produced the canonical layer-2 parts), not to a source or a column individually. Grounded in rfr validation.json's top-level cross-source keys.

source_overlap_s.description: Seconds during which two or more sources overlapped in time (the window in which cross-source agreement could be measured).

hr_mean_abs_diff_bpm.description: Mean absolute difference in heart rate (bpm) between two sources over their overlap. Absent when fewer than two sources reported HR.

video_sync.description: Per-clip camera-clock agreement against the telemetry timeline.

file.description: Footage file this sync fact is about.

camera_drift_s.description: Estimated camera-clock drift (s) against the telemetry timeline. null when it could not be estimated.

prev_gap_s.description: Gap (s) between the previous clip's end and this clip's start. null for the first clip.

warnings.description: Human-readable refine-time merge-quality warnings emitted by the producer (e.g. low per-source coverage). Free text; carried verbatim.

source.description: Source id used as the shadow-column / provenance namespace (e.g. garmin, bosch).

file.description: Original file name of the raw source (the layer-1 artifact this provenance points at).

window.description: Time window this source actually covers.

raw_points.description: Number of raw samples in this source.

clock_sync_offset_s.description: Offset (seconds) applied to align this source's clock onto the canonical UTC timeline. 0 = reference source.

dual_hr_anchor.description: Optional cross-source anchor used when two sources both report HR: the correlation lag/quality used to lock their clocks together.

hash.description: sha256 of the raw source's original bytes, algorithm-prefixed 'sha256:<hex>'. Enables dedup, integrity, and duplicate-import detection. REQUIRED per source.

column_coverage.description: Coverage of one canonical column against its feeding source, with gaps classified as source_silent vs field_missing (grounded in rfr validate.py).

source.description: Provenance as 'source.field' (e.g. garmin.gps, bosch.power). The source that supplied this canonical column.

fallback.description: Optional 'source.field' that fills where the primary is silent (e.g. moving = bosch.distance with garmin.speed fallback). null when the column is a 1:1 passthrough.

missing_s.description: Seconds within the span with no value.

coverage_pct.description: Percentage of the span that has a value.

kind.description: source_silent = the source wrote no records at all during the gap (e.g. a pause); field_missing = records kept coming but this field was absent (a real dropout, e.g. HR strap lost).
